description_long: This object is a sculpted chaise in the form of a reclining classical female figure, reminiscent of Greco-Roman art. The figure is draped in flowing robes, with intricate folds and realistic detailing in the fabric, and is positioned on her side atop a rectangular base. Her upper body is slightly propped up by her left arm, while her right arm rests across her lap, and her legs are extended with one knee slightly bent. The head is turned to the side, with detailed hair styled in a classical bun. The entire piece appears to be made from terracotta or a similar reddish-brown ceramic material, giving it a matte, earthy finish. The chaise is both decorative and functional, serving as a sculptural seat or urban ornament.
description: A reclining classical female figure sculpted in terracotta, draped in detailed robes and resting on a rectangular base. The piece combines artistic ornamentation with the form of a chaise.
description_view_0: Side view showing the reclining figure's back, draped robes, and the full length of the chaise's base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the figure's feet, the flow of the robes, and the curvature of the back and base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the figure's face, bent arm, and the detailed folds of the garment.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the head, shoulders, and arm placement, with a clear view of the upper torso and base.
attribute_backrest_design: The backrest is formed by the upper torso and arm of the reclining figure, gently sloping and following the natural curve of the body. It is not segmented and features no additional decorative elements beyond the sculpted drapery.
attribute_armrest_presence: The left arm of the figure acts as an integrated armrest, while the right side is open with no armrest present.
attribute_leg_style: The chaise does not have traditional legs; instead, the base is a solid rectangular slab that supports the entire sculpted figure. The figure's own legs are part of the artistic design and are not functional supports.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: There is no upholstery; the surface is sculpted to resemble draped fabric, with detailed folds and a solid, unpatterned texture typical of terracotta.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: No separate cushions are present; the chaise is a single sculpted piece with the appearance of draped fabric providing the only visual suggestion of softness.
